question: Can off-site backup be completed without inventing credentials?
answer: NO — no real off-site destination is configured or discoverable.

probes.result: only authorized_keys; no storage_box key, no ssh config Host for storagebox
BACKUP_STORAGE_BOX_SSH empty or unset; no usable secret exposed to agent
pncp_datalake-*.dump under /var/lib/extra-consultoria/backups/postgresql/ — same host; fail-closed offsite status correctly refuses

allowed_alternatives_documented: Object storage adapted from backup-database.sh (not implemented as drop-in today)
not_done: Did not claim local dump as off-site
Did not set REQUIRE_STORAGE_BOX=1 while empty
operator_action_required: See UNBLOCK.md — set BACKUP_STORAGE_BOX_SSH on VPS only, authorize key, mount, run backup + restore-from-remote drill
unblock_test: python3 -m scripts.ops.campaign_offsite_backup_status → status ok/configured; then restore from remote package
